failure_summary: The tier_loader_v4 mission ratified successfully according to the queue and benchmark gates, but produced unusable code due to two compounding failures the benchmark runner did not detect.

primary_failures.evidence: Proposal ends at line 332 with 'def select_sprinter(self) -> List[ModelInfo]:' and has no method body. Multiple required functions (select_cruiser, select_architect, load_tier_mapping, get_model_for_tier, describe_mapping) are entirely missing.
primary_failures.root_cause: qwen3.5:9b cruiser tier ran out of context window before completing the implementation of a brief that specified 6 dataclasses + 7 functions in ~6000 bytes.

primary_failures.evidence: Brief specified module-level functions returning a TierMapping dataclass. Proposal wrote class-based version with methods on an unspecified parent class. Model also hallucinated the name 'YouMapping' in its preamble before any code.
primary_failures.root_cause: 9B model misunderstood the architectural style. No prompt-level reinforcement of 'module-level functions, NOT a class' in the brief.

secondary_failures.evidence: The benchmark runner gave PASS to a proposal that was missing more than half its required surface area and violated the brief's architectural contract.
secondary_failures.root_cause: Benchmark runner only validates: syntactic parseability, no hallucinated imports, pytest collection. It does NOT validate: presence of all expected names from the brief, presence of all expected dataclasses, or completeness of function bodies.
secondary_failures.impact: P15 was marked RATIFIED in the queue, leading to a false sense of forward progress. The contaminated tier_loader.py from auto_deploy's separate bug masked this for a full day.

for_rewrite: Split the brief into TWO smaller missions: (1) tier_loader_v5_dataclasses (just ModelInfo + TierMapping) and (2) tier_loader_v5_selectors (the function suite).
Use the heavy tier (post-MoE swap) instead of cruiser. The 9B context window is too small for this brief in one shot.
Add explicit anti-pattern warnings to the brief: 'Do NOT define a class. Do NOT use methods. These are module-level functions, period.'
Include a worked example of the WRONG style being rejected, alongside the RIGHT style.
Provide a concrete usage example showing how callers will invoke select_heavy(models) → str.
for_forge_system: Add brief-contract validation gate to the benchmark runner: extract expected names (classes, functions, dataclasses) from the brief's INTERFACE CONTRACT section, verify each is defined in the proposal.
Add truncation detection: flag any proposal whose final non-blank line is a function/class/method signature without a body.
Add minimum-substance threshold per mission type: complex ARCHITECTURE missions should require N defined names, M non-blank code lines.
Track context-window utilization in the proposal frontmatter so escalations can show 'model produced 4096/4096 tokens — likely truncation'.
for_e14_self_healing_engine: This failure mode becomes the seeded pattern BENCHMARK_FALSE_POSITIVE_TRUNCATION in the E14 failure pattern library. Detection signature: proposal compiles, benchmark passes, but expected_markers verification fails.
Resolution action for this pattern: REWRITE_BRIEF (split into smaller pieces) + CHANGE_TIER (escalate to heavy).
